data.epi_content:
Erica took a bite of the cookie on the table after sending Vivian away.

It tasted excellent, just like before.

This time, instead of waiting for Evan to visit my room, I went directly to his room.

After all, it's usually the person who has something to say or who feels regretful that does the visiting.

"Evan, it's me. Open the door."

At those words, the door opened as if it were automatic, without anyone coming out.

As I walked in, I caught a slightly fragrant scent of medicinal ingredients mixed with the distinctive smell of books.

Since all the available seating was already occupied by countless books and medicine bottles, I sat on the bed and told Evan about the conversation I'd just had with Vivian.

"For a while, we can have a peaceful time—the kind Vivian likes."

"I suppose so."

Evan was still flipping through a book, muttering some kind of incantation.

I could see something resembling a human crying for help in a small flask, but it wasn't of much interest to me, so I ignored it.

If it had been a cute dog inside, I might have paid more attention.

Most humans tend to feel more sorrow when dogs die miserably than when people die like dogs.

"Then for a while, we just need to avoid being caught.

Like before, chatting and pretending to study a bit should be enough.

Oh, I was just about to go into the city for a harvest. Want to come along?"

Evan, still scribbling something and muttering to himself, asked if I wanted to join him.

I responded with a slightly teasing tone.

"Is that a date invitation?"

"Maybe?"

"I don't think it's particularly enjoyable as a date activity.

It smells, it's damp, and though they'll soon be dead, we'll have to meet some unpleasant people."

It's called a harvest, but it's more like capturing knife-wielding thugs from back alleys or magicians from criminal organizations and boiling them down like beef bone soup.

The difference is that the ingredients aren't fresh, while those being boiled are the very essence of freshness, still moving around.

"What can I do? To avoid complications, we can only touch those kinds of people."

"Don't worry, I won't smoke in your room."

I said, waving the half-burned tobacco in my hand.

Since Evan didn't seem to like it much, I had put it out before entering.

I would have sprayed some perfume if I had any, but I don't particularly like it, so I didn't have any.

"I'm just feeling a bit anxious today, so I want to sleep here.

Being with Vivian doesn't exactly bring back good memories."

"...Alright."

At those words, Evan stared at me for quite a while before answering and then left the room.

.

.

.

.

.

From the next day on, as if nothing had happened, I started attending classes, hanging out with Evan and Vivian around the Academy, playing and studying together.
data.epi_content2:
Since there weren't any brazen friends like Lydia in the class yet, when I returned to the classroom after a long absence, some people just whispered among themselves, but no one approached me.

Thanks to that, I could recall the old days—when I lived a relatively normal life.

Peacefully drinking tea, eating sweets, talking with friends, and imagining returning to my family, marrying Evan, watching our prosperous family, and growing old together.

Time passed quite quickly.

About one, two, three months went by.

Since I wasn't tied up in Vivian's room, unable to leave and being kept like a pet, it wasn't that difficult to get along with Vivian and Evan.

Occasionally, when my breathing became rapid due to stress, smoking tobacco was enough to calm me down.

Even when the atmosphere became awkward because of something I said, Vivian would pretend not to hear it and move on as if she knew nothing, allowing this precarious daily life to continue.

I thought everything would continue without any problems.

Well, to put it differently, I thought Vivian wouldn't catch me killing someone in the Academy and disposing of the body.

If not for that, our daily life, which only appeared precarious from the outside, would have continued as long as Vivian's feelings weren't hurt.

It might have continued if I hadn't been sitting on a corpse, counting bullets.

I wonder how she got into my room when there was no smell of blood and all the doors were tightly closed.

"Erica? Evan...? What on earth are you two doing?"

As Evan gathered black magic in his hands, seemingly about to shoot it at Vivian, I quickly responded.

There's no need to fight, right?

I'm not sure if we can resolve this through conversation, but...

"I was almost raped. Evan saved me."

"...Are you joking?"

"I told you before, fallen nobles like me are treated much worse than talented commoners like you.

This is just an extension of that kind of thing."

Vivian heard my answer and stared at the corpse for a long time.

For someone who supposedly tried to rape me, the fact that the body was contaminated with black magic and dead might seem a bit questionable.

I thought Vivian might just believe me, so I tried it, but of course she wouldn't.

I was just trying to buy some time.

"Erica, I'm, I'm not a fool.

I just want to accept what my friend says."

Vivian said this while gathering magical power in the air.

Simultaneously, countless semi-transparent wind blades and fireballs appeared in the air.

"Many people have disappeared lately.

Lydia, Ella, Joanna, and an unnamed maid who entered the lady's room and never came out—I knew about all of this but didn't ask any questions.

Their last known location was here, but they simply disappeared."

I wonder if it was only four.

"Are you going to cut off my head with that wind and burn the corpse?

Burning feels rather terrible, so I'd rather not experience it."

I say with a slightly bitter feeling.

Being a lover of peace, I tend to avoid violence and cruelty.

"Tell Evan to disperse his magic first."

Why is she telling me?

He would listen if I told him, but still.
data.epi_content3:
"Miss, black magic is evil.

To gain power, you must kill countless humans, and it corrupts even the user.

It turns people into beings who are not swayed by human compassion and are obsessed only with their beliefs and the price they pay."

Well, the humans here are so full of compassion that if my head were to fall off, they would try to catch my blood and grab my skin, head, and bones.

Isn't that closer to black magic?

"It's still better than gods who give without requiring any price in return."

It was Evan who answered.

Vivian, with a slightly tearful face, shedding tears from her right eye, asks us:

"Just yesterday, we were laughing, having fun conversations, and even eating together. Was it all an act?

Were you doing these horrible things behind my back while smiling with me?"

She probably doesn't think we'll feel guilty or stop our evil deeds just because she says that.

If this situation could be resolved through conversation, we would have dispersed the spells we're aiming at each other long ago.

I don't answer.

Vivian knew this well, so she didn't ask anymore either.

She just sighed once, as if slightly disappointed.

Or as if she knew this would happen soon, she raised her hands and gathered even more spells, deploying numerous magic attacks as if it were a joke.

I thought that if she sent those at me, I might die without a trace.

"Well, I kind of suspected it. Your mouth was smiling, but it felt like I was the only one excited about our conversations.

I always felt like I was being left out, but I was secretly happy thinking you two might be in love again.

Who would have known you'd become mosquitoes sucking magic from corpses?"

I pointed my pistol at Vivian and pulled the trigger.

Of course, it was futile—the bullet was blocked by an invisible barrier, pathetically crumpled, and fell to the floor with a clang.

And the face of Vivian, who had been mocking me until just now, hardened.

Perhaps with that face, saying "This is what God desires," and launching those deadly spells, it would look like an angel driving out a demon.

While I was lost in such idle thoughts, Evan quickly shot his gathered spell at Vivian and threw himself toward me.

As my body began to blur, all those spells flew toward me.

I wonder if Evan was just showing off.

How could anyone win against that?

As my left foot disappeared without a trace, I felt myself being transported somewhere.

And in that moment, our eyes met.

I suddenly wonder what emotions Vivian might be feeling.

She maintained an expressionless face as if nothing bothered her, exuding an aura like an absolute being punishing black magicians, but her eyes were slightly moist, as if about to shed tears.

Then my vision flickered, and I opened my eyes on a bed in a house I'd never seen before.

The white bed began to turn red.

"Evan, my left foot is gone."

I simply state the fact calmly.

Without any reproach like "Didn't you say you'd protect me?"

Such promises tend to lose their effect when spoken aloud.

So, from within my pocket, as if sending down curses with smoke when they rise up, I put tobacco in my mouth, lit it, and spoke again:

"Everything important just disappears."

Whether it's conscience, manners, or common sense.

I'm tired.

The Crown Prince, Vivian, whatever.
